title: Workflow Definition
description: Contract schema for durable workflow DEFINITIONS (as opposed to workflow runs). A workflow definition declares: the workflow_id + workflow_version identity, a sequence of step definitions (each with an actor, optional adapter binding, required capabilities, on_failure semantic, and optional governance gate), the expected set of adapters the run will invoke, default policies to apply at run start, and intent-classifier tags. Run-time cross-reference validation with the adapter manifest registry is opt-in via WorkflowRegistry.validate_cross_refs; PR-A3 executor is the canonical fail-closed consumer.

workflow_id.description: Workflow identifier. Same pattern as workflow-run.schema.v1.json::workflow_id for cross-reference alignment. Drift regression test asserts pattern equality.

display_name.description: Human-readable label shown in CLI and docs.

steps.description: Ordered step sequence. Executor (PR-A3) drives steps in declared order, branching per on_failure semantic.

expected_adapter_refs.description: Adapter IDs this workflow expects to invoke. Pattern matches agent-adapter-contract.schema.v1.json::adapter_id. Cross-reference validation checks every adapter manifest exists and satisfies required_capabilities.

default_policy_refs.description: Policies automatically attached to runs of this workflow at run start. Typically includes policy_worktree_profile.v1.json.

required_capabilities.description: Workflow-level capability requirements (union over all adapter steps). Cross-ref check: at least one expected adapter must supply each listed capability.

tags.description: Intent-classifier hints. Rules matching these tags boost classification confidence.

capability_enum.description: Mirrors agent-adapter-contract.schema.v1.json::$defs/capability_enum. Drift regression test asserts set equality.

step_name.description: Step identifier within this workflow. Unique within steps[] (loader enforces; not JSON-Schema representable).

actor.description: Who performs this step. adapter: external coding agent. ao-kernel: internal orchestrator. human: HITL gate. system: automated infra (CI, timeouts).

adapter_id.description: Adapter bound to this step. Required when actor=adapter (schema conditional below). Must be in workflow.expected_adapter_refs (loader-level cross-ref).

required_capabilities.description: Capabilities the bound adapter must advertise. Subset check at cross-reference validation.

policy_refs.description: Additional policies active only for this step (union with workflow.default_policy_refs at run start).

on_failure.description: Failure handling policy. transition_to_failed: move run to terminal failed state with error.category mapped to the failure kind. retry_once: exactly one immediate retry, NO backoff, same input envelope; if the retry also fails, transition_to_failed applies. escalate_to_human: open a HITL approval gate with diagnostic payload; human may grant retry or deny (which transitions to failed). PR-A2 v1 enum is closed; retry_with_backoff, branch_to_alternative, continue_on_error, and custom are intentionally NOT included (FAZ-D #9 native branching scope).

timeout_seconds.description: Step-level wall-clock deadline. Executor computes effective_deadline = min(timeout_seconds, remaining run-level budget.time_seconds). On step-level miss: error.category='timeout'. On run-level budget miss: error.category='budget_exhausted'. Absent → only run-level budget applies.

human_interrupt_allowed.description: Whether the bound adapter may raise an interrupt_request mid-step. Requires the adapter capability human_interrupt.

gate.description: Governance gate BEFORE this step executes. Run transitions to waiting_approval; resume via approval_token (separate from adapter interrupt_token domain).

operation.description: Internal primitive identifier for actor=ao-kernel (context_compile, patch_*) or actor=system (ci_*) steps. REQUIRED when actor in {ao-kernel, system}; NOT ALLOWED when actor in {adapter, human}. context_compile runs the PR-A? context pipeline (start_session + compile_context + process_turn / DEMO-SCRIPT step 3). patch_preview validates via `git apply --check --3way --index -` without side effects. patch_apply runs `git apply --3way --index -` and writes a reverse-diff to {run_dir}/patches/{patch_id}.revdiff via atomic write. patch_rollback replays the reverse diff; idempotent when the worktree is already clean. ci_pytest / ci_ruff / ci_mypy invoke hermetic subprocesses under policy_worktree_profile.v1 env/command/cwd guards. Introduced in PR-A4.
